title: Entrepreneurship
objective: Students will explore entrepreneurship concepts including Making Something to Sell, Who Is a Customer?, Buyers & Sellers and more.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Making Something to Sell!
The experience of creating a product to sell — a craft, a drawing, baked goods; what makes something worth buying; pride in making something others want
Let me show you something cool. Create a simple product (craft, drawing, baked item) that could be sold
If you made something at home — like friendship bracelets or decorated biscuits — could they explain why someone might want to buy them?

Great job! Today we learned about Making Something to Sell. Create a simple product (craft, drawing, baked item) that could be sold Explain what makes their product good enough for someone to want to buy it
Let's explore Who Is a Customer?! Have you ever wondered about this?
Understanding that a customer is someone who buys what you make or do; thinking about what customers want and need; the idea that businesses serve people
Let's practice together! Define what a customer is in their own words
If you was planning to sell lemonade at a school event, could they think about what would make people want to buy their lemonade rather than someone else's?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Who Is a Customer?: Define what a customer is in their own words Suggest what a customer might look for when deciding whether to buy something (quality, price, looks nice)
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Buyers & Sellers!
When someone makes or has something, they can sell it; when someone wants something, they can buy it; introduction to the concept of exchange and transactions
Let me show you something cool. Explain what a buyer does and what a seller does
If you set up a stall at a school fair, could they explain who the buyers and sellers are and what each person does?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Buyers & Sellers. Explain what a buyer does and what a seller does Act out a simple buying and selling transaction with a partner
Today we're going to learn about Goods & Services. Get ready for an adventure!
Some people make things (goods) and some people do things for others (services); recognising both in everyday life and understanding that both have value
Time to try this out: Sort a set of pictures into goods (things you can touch) and services (things people do for you)
Could you tell you the difference between a product you buy in a shop and a service like getting a haircut, and give examples of each?

Great job! Today we learned about Goods & Services. Sort a set of pictures into goods (things you can touch) and services (things people do for you) Give three examples of goods and three examples of services from their everyday life
